pundit.name: Eric Wynalda
pundit.take: **Mustard Boy's draft was a comedy of errors, and handing him Ghana to face England is like giving a toddler the keys to a Ferrari.** Let's be clear: England have form, talent, and a manager who actually understands the basics of team selection. Meanwhile, Mustard Boy, a.k.a. Zach, might have picked his teams based on a FIFA video game's popularity list rather than actual footballing logic. Ghana's going to be exposed under the spotlight, and it's going to be brutal. Gayden—oh, excuse me, "The Backpass Assassin"—despite being dead last, will relish this chance to outmaneuver his amateur rival. England takes this one, no question about it—Ghana's toast, and Zach will need more than mustard to cover up this mess.

pundit_takes.headline: Gunner's DR Congo Set to Upset Tournament with Tactical Genius
pundit_takes.subtitle: 'Bubba G' channels spirit of underdogs in Group K showdown.

pundit_takes.headline: Gayden's England to Face Zach's Ghana in Tactical Showpiece
pundit_takes.subtitle: The Backpass Assassin hopes to move out of last place.

pundit_takes.headline: Rafe Quietly Climbs Standings with Germany, Proves Drafting is Overrated
pundit_takes.subtitle: The Noisemaker silently outperforms expectations despite questionable selections.

pundit_takes.headline: Devin Inches Closer to Top, Thanks to Norway's Grit Against Senegal
pundit_takes.subtitle: Ghost Pepper's heat rises with each Nordic triumph in the standings.

rome_analytics_quotes: Zach is on a losing streak, but Devin surged past with a W3 heater.
Rafe's draft is so bad, it's a miracle he’s not dead last.
Rafe can't beat Gunner, showing The Backpass Assassin Jr. still needs some training wheels.
Devin's underdog surge proves the model's predictions need a serious tune-up.
